Details of experiments and data analysis procedure.

Heavy residues produced in the target were all
strongly forward focused due to the inverse reaction
kinematics.They were identified using the fragment
The elements below terbium were identified from
an energy loss measurement in an ionisation chamber.
The data of the present work were corrected as
explained in REL-REF,  where a more detailed
description on the corrections applied can be found.
Here only a short reminder is given.
Corrections on the production rates determined from
velocity distributions included dead-time of the
data-acquisition system and detector efficiencies. The
dead-time was kept below 30%, and the efficiency of the
detectors was determined to be higher than 98%.  In
addition, losses due to non-fully stripped ions, due to
secondary reactions in the FRS beam line, and due to
limited angular acceptance were taken into account.
The correction for secondary reactions in the
liquid-deuterium target was performed both for the
using a fast simplified version of the CASCABLA code.
This is described in more detailed in the appendix of
